notes: Bank Eqtesad-e Novin; license/ops date 2001; Established 2000 as part of government's banking privatization; widely cited as Iran's first private bank of the post-revolution privatization wave; began commercial operations 2001 [source: Wikipedia EN Bank; WebSearch aggregation of company histories]
Bank Karafarin; license/ops date 2001; Among the first cohort of banks entering the network from 2001 onward alongside Parsian and Eqtesad Novin [source: Wikipedia Karafarin Bank]
Bank Parsian; license/ops date 2002-01; Incorporated September 2001; commenced operations January 2002 after Central Bank of Iran issued its banking license [source: Wikipedia Parsian Bank]

Bank Saman; license/ops date 2002-08; Received full banking license and adopted the name Saman Bank in August 2002 (had operated in a more limited financial-institution form prior) [source: Iran Watch / WebSearch aggregation]
Bank Pasargad; license/ops date 2005-09-13; Established as Iran's fifth private bank under a Central Bank of Iran license dated September 13, 2005 [source: Wikipedia Bank Pasargad]
Bank Sarmayeh; license/ops date ; Founded 2005 [source: Wikipedia Sarmayeh Bank]

Bank Sina; license/ops date 2001-2011; Operating by the end of the 2001-2011 first-wave-of-eight-banks period per Iran1400.org; exact founding year not independently confirmed beyond that range [source: Iran1400.org]

Bank Tat; license/ops date ; Founded 2009 by businessman Ali Ansari with initial capital ~2 trillion rials; later merged into Ayandeh Bank (2013) [source: WebSearch aggregation (Wikipedia/Grokipedia Ayandeh Bank cross-check)]
Bank Dey; license/ops date 2001-2011; Operating by end of the 2001-2011 first-wave-of-eight-banks period per Iran1400.org; exact founding year not independently confirmed [source: Iran1400.org]
Bank Shahr; license/ops date 2001-2011; Operating by end of the 2001-2011 first-wave-of-eight-banks period per Iran1400.org; exact founding year not independently confirmed [source: Iran1400.org]

Bank Ayandeh; license/ops date 2015-03-17; Established 2013 via merger of Bank Tat with the state-linked Salehin Credit Institution and Atee Credit Institution; received its own CBI operating license (no. 93/349144) March 17 2015; license REVOKED and bank dissolved/merged into Bank Melli on 2025-10-23 following a corruption/mismanagement scandal -- included as the most recent bookend of the post-2001 private-bank cycle [source: Wikipedia Ayandeh Bank; Al Jazeera 2025-10-27 'Corruption, mismanagement in spotlight as Iran dissolves major private bank']

; license/ops date ; Per Iran1400.org: 'until 2011, seven additional banks launched' beyond the Karafarin/Parsian/Eqtesad-Novin first cohort -- named as Pasargad, Saman, Sarmayeh, Sina, Tat, Dey, Shahr (all individually rowed above except this summary note) [source: Iran1400.org]
